LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF JANET PICKENS' MOTION TO RECONSIDER DISMISSAL OF CASE AND MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT OF 09/10/08, AND TO EXTEND FURNISH SECURITY ORDER TO 10/01/08 IS DENIED. COURT CANNOT RECONSIDER ITS ORDER BECAUSE JUDGMENT HAS BEEN ENTERED. SEE APRI INS. CO. V. SUP. CRT., (1999) 76 CAL. APP. 4TH 176, 180. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY-JACKIE ROBINSON APARTMENT'S MOTION FOR ORDER REQUIRING PLAINTIFF TO FURNISH SECURITY CCP 391.1 IS GRANTED. THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE COMPLAINT AND THE MOVING PAPERS SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANT PURSUANT TO MORAN V. MURTAUGH MILLER MEYER & NELSON, LLP (2007) 40 CAL. 4TH 780. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NO PROBABILITY OF SUCCESS IN THIS ACTION IS REQUIRED TO POST A BOND OF $500.00. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-14-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to Defendant's Counsel for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is ordered off calendar/Case removed from Early Settlement Program. Proceedings not reported. (212)

Notice Of Motion To Reconsider Dismissal Of Case And Motion To Vacate Judgment Of 09/10/08, And Ot Extend Furnish Security Order To 10/01/08
Off Calendar Oct-08-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Janet Pickens (648.1579) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona
Ryan Walton of Zanghi Torres & Arshawsky (977.0444) for defendant and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF JANET PICKENS' MOTION TO RECONSIDER DISMISSAL OF CASE AND MOTION TO VACATE JUDGMENT OF 09/10/08, AND TO EXTEND FURNISH SECURITY ORDER TO 10/01/08 is denied. Court cannot reconsider its order because judgment has been entered. See APRI Ins. Co. v. Sup. Crt., (1999) 76 Cal. App. 4th 176, 180. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring Pltf To Furnish Security Ccp 391.1; (P/A, Request For Judicial Notice, Declaration)Ccp 128.7
Off Calendar Aug-07-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Paul Arshawsky of Zanghi Torres Arshawsky (977.0444) for defendants and moving parties

No other appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY-JACKIE ROBINSON APARTMENT'S MOTION FOR ORDER REQUIRING PLAINTIFF TO FURNISH SECURITY CCP 391.1 is granted. The Court has reviewed the complaint and the moving papers submitted by defendant pursuant to Moran v. Murtaugh Miller Meyer & Nelson, LLP (2007) 40 Cal. 4th 780. The Court finds that plaintiff has no probability of success in this action is required to post a bond of $500.00. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Feb-07-2008 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.
Plaintiff Janet Pickens, a/k/a Janet Stanford, was present in pro per, placed under oath and testified.
No apppearance by defendant The John Stewart Company-Jackie Robinson Apartments.

Default entered against defendant The John Stewart Company-Jackie Robinson Apartments - 01/09/08.
Plaintiff failed to serve statement of damages and reservation of right to seek punitive damages on defendant pursuant to CCP 425.11 & CCP 425.115, respectively.

Matter taken Off Calendar.
